_doc: Per-user partition spec, one entry per data-user (good + bad + freerider).
Activate by setting partition_strategy='per_user' + per_user_partitions='<this path>'
in ExperimentConfiguration.
Two accepted JSON shapes:
Multi-dataset (this file): {user_index: {name, datasets: {DATASET: spec}}}.
Active dataset is picked from experiment_config.dataset at runtime.
Dataset keys are case-insensitive ('MNIST'/'mnist'/'CIFAR-10'/'cifar-10' all OK).
Single-dataset (legacy): {users: [...]} or {user_index: spec}, with the same
fields flat on each user. Used as fallback when no dataset-specific entry
matches the active dataset.
Semantics: each user takes a fair stratified share of every class equal to
data_percent of the dataset, then drops samples whose label is not in the
user's whitelist (only_labels or label_distribution.keys()). data_percent
stays a stable mental model regardless of skew. A user with pct=10% and
only_labels=[4,9] effectively trains on ~2% of dataset (2 of 10 classes
kept) -- bump pct if you want full coverage on those classes.
Fields per user:
name               (str)              Human-readable label, kept on the user
for logs/plots. Optional.
datasets           (dict)             Dataset name -> spec dict (multi-dataset
shape). Each spec dict carries:
data_percent       (float, required)  Fair share of dataset before filtering.
Sum across users <= 100 per dataset.
In overlap mode each fair share is taken
from the rep-inflated class pools.
only_labels        (list[int])        Hard whitelist. Drops every class
outside the list after fair-share.
label_distribution (dict[int->float]) Per-class retention factor in [0, 1]
applied to the fair share. 1.0 keeps
all, 0.5 keeps half, 0.0 drops the
class. Unmentioned classes default
to 1.0. Combine with only_labels to
drop some + downsample others.
flip_map           (dict[int->int])   Malicious label-flip map applied at
dataset read time.
Sum of data_percent per dataset must be <= 100. Skewed users keep less,
never conflict. Number of entries must equal number_of_good_contributors +
number_of_bad_contributors + number_of_freerider_contributors.
Example below: 8 data-users, MNIST and CIFAR-10 profiles. User 0/1 carry
only_labels skew, user 2 is malicious (flip_map), user 3 has retention
weights, rest stratified.
